rulings.Case Title: CLAYTON HARMSTON et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Motion For Leave To File Motion To Tax Costs Out Of Time And Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 25, 2011, line 13, Plaintiffs Motion For Leave To File Motion To Tax Costs Out Of Time And Motion For Reconsideration. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO TAX COSTS AND MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION ARE DENIED. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION IS UNTIMELY AND PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW NEW FACTS OR LAW TO SUPPORT RECONSIDERATION. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JEREMIAH M CADIGAN VS. TERRY W FONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Terry W. Fong And Betsy C. Fong, Dba 7-Eleven Store'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff'S Ex Pert Richard Corriea With Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 25, 2011, line 5, Defendants Terry W. Fong And Betsy C. Fong, Dba 7-Eleven Store's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff's ExPert Richard Corriea With Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT RICHARD CORRIEA TO TAKE PLACE BY FRIDAY, MAY 6. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,375 TO BE PAID BY PLAINTIFF TO DEFENDANT WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN WHITEHOUSE VS. PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Protective Order, To Quash Subpoenas Of Pltf Susan Whitehouse'S Medical Records, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 25, 2011, line 1, PLAINTIFF SUSAN WHITEHOUSE Motion For Protective Order, To Quash Subpoenas Of Plaintiff Susan Whitehouse's Medical Records, And Request For Sanctions. MOTION TO QUASH DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS ORDERED TO PAY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000.00 TO DEFENSE COUNSEL WITHIN 20 DAYS AFTER NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JANINE BOCCIARDI VS. APPEARANCE ENHANCEMENT CLINIC, A MEDICAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 25, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANT FRED SUESS, M.D. DBA APPEARANCE ENHANCEMENT CLINIC DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. A COMPLAINT CAN CONTAIN INCONSISTENT OR ALTERNATIVE THEORIES OF LIABILITY THAT ARISE OUT OF THE SAME SET OF FACTS. CROWLEY V. KATLEMAN (1994) 8 CAL.4TH 666, 690-691. PLAINTIFF HAS ALSO PLED FACTS WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO SUPPORT HER CAUSES OF ACTION. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 25, 2011, line 5, CROSS-DEFENDANT DALE SCOTT & CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ACTING BY AND VS. HAIGHT-ASHBURY NEIGHBORHOOD COUNCIL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Haight-Ashbury Neighborhood Council'S Demurrer To Complaint (Advanced From May-2 To Apr-25)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 25, 2011, line 7, Defendant Haight-Ashbury Neighborhood Council's Demurrer To Complaint. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. MURRIETA VALLEY UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT V. COUNTY OF RIVERSIDE IS CONTROLLING ON THE ISSUE OF VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS OF PUBLIC ENTITIES. SECOND, AT THIS POINT IN THE LITIGATION, THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE WHEN THE LEASE ACTUALLY EXPIRED AND WHEN OR WHETHER IT WAS RENEWED. DEFENDANT MUST FILE ITS ANSWER WITHIN 5 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. =(302/LMG)
